name: Serra de São Mamede Natural Park Loop
name_original: Anello gravel da Castelo de Vide tra massi erratici
description: This difficult 45-mile gravel loop from Castelo de Vide explores ancient megalithic sites and medieval towns with 6097 feet of climbing.
region_intro: Gravel biking around Santa Maria Da Devesa offers diverse landscapes within Portugal's Alentejo region. The area features rolling hills and the mountainous terrain of the Serra de São Mamede Natural Park, providing varied elevation changes suitable for gravel cycling. Routes traverse a mix of natural environments and historical sites, including ancient megalithic monuments. This region is characterized by its blend of challenging paths and scenic routes, appealing to gravel bikers seeking both adventure and cultural exploration. Best gravel bike trails around Santa Maria Da Devesa The most popular gravel bike trail is Gravel loop from Castelo de Vide, a 59.7 miles (96.1 km) trail that takes 8 hours 37 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the varied terrain surrounding Castelo de Vide. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is Serra de São Mamede Natural Park Loop, a difficult 45.0 miles (72.4 km) path. This route offers extensive views and challenging climbs within the natural park. Local gravel bikers also love the Marvão Castle – Castelo de Vide loop from Marvão, a 52.8 miles (85.0 km) trail leading through mountainous landscapes and past historic villages, often completed in about 7 hours 53 minutes. Gravel biking around Santa Maria Da Devesa is defined by the Serra de São Mamede mountains, rolling hills, and ancient megalithic monuments. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 14 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Santa Maria Da Devesa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many gravel bike trails are available around Santa Maria Da Devesa?
A: There are 5 gravel bike trails available for exploration around Santa Maria Da Devesa, offering a range of experiences within the Alentejo region.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of gravel biking routes in Santa Maria Da Devesa?
A: The gravel biking routes around Santa Maria Da Devesa are predominantly challenging, with 4 difficult trails and 1 moderate trail. This makes the area particularly appealing to experienced gravel bikers seeking varied terrain and elevation changes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly gravel biking routes in the area?
A: While most routes are rated difficult, there is one moderate trail, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2332252020/route-21-povoa-e-meadas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route 21 Póvoa e Meadas&lt;/a&gt;, which might be more suitable for families or those looking for a less strenuous ride. It covers 17.9 km with moderate elevation changes.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see on the gravel bike trails?
A: Gravel biking around Santa Maria Da Devesa offers diverse landscapes, from the rolling hills of the Alentejo to the mountainous terrain of the Serra de São Mamede Natural Park. You'll encounter lush greenery, scenic views, and a mix of natural environments.

Q: Are there any circular gravel bike routes available?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops. For example, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2330400699/gravel-loop-from-castelo-de-vide" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gravel loop from Castelo de Vide&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2330426414/serra-de-sao-mamede-natural-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra de São Mamede Natural Park Loop&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32128240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marvão Castle – Castelo de Vide loop from Marvão&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical landmarks or natural monuments can I visit along the routes?
A: The region is rich in history and natural beauty. You can encounter ancient megalithic monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1133380" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Menir da Meada&lt;/a&gt; (the largest in the Iberian Peninsula) and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5324586" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen Tapada de Anta&lt;/a&gt;. Historic sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/659213" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Village of Marvão&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/795936" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marvão Castle&lt;/a&gt; are also prominent features.

Q: What do other gravel bikers enjoy most about the trails in Santa Maria Da Devesa?
A: The komoot community praises the diverse terrain and scenic beauty of the region. Many appreciate the challenging paths within the Serra de São Mamede Natural Park and the opportunity to combine adventure with cultural exploration of historic villages and ancient monuments.

Q: Are there any routes that offer extensive views or viewpoints?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2330426414/serra-de-sao-mamede-natural-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra de São Mamede Natural Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; is known for offering extensive views across the natural park. The mountainous terrain throughout the region provides numerous opportunities for panoramic vistas of the Alentejo landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year to go gravel biking in Santa Maria Da Devesa?
A: The Alentejo region, including Santa Maria Da Devesa, can be quite warm in summer. Spring and autumn generally offer more pleasant temperatures and lush greenery, making them ideal seasons for gravel biking. Winter can also be suitable, though some paths might be wetter.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through or near historic villages?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32128240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marvão Castle – Castelo de Vide loop from Marvão&lt;/a&gt; leads through mountainous landscapes and past historic villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/659213" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marvão&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/401156" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castelo de Vide&lt;/a&gt;, allowing you to experience the region's rich heritage.

Q: Can I find routes that include a visit to the Serra de São Mamede Natural Park?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2330426414/serra-de-sao-mamede-natural-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra de São Mamede Natural Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to explore this significant natural feature, offering challenging climbs and diverse paths within the park's picturesque scenery.

Q: Are there any routes that start or end near a train station for public transport access?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31502314" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castelo de Vide – Beirã Train Station loop from Castelo de Vide&lt;/a&gt; offers a route that connects to the Beirã Train Station, providing an option for those using public transport to access the trails.
tips: Urban centre and municipal headquarters. Town situated on a hill on the border of Alentejo. Medieval town under the jurisdiction of a religious-military order (Order of Christ) with castle and urban enclosure. 17th-century war square integrated into the defence line of the Alentejo border.

It's a hard climb up to Marvao and our route took us us v steep cobbles. Fine on mountain bike but hard on fully laden tandem. The main road will also get you there. Either way views from Marvao and the town itself are fabulous!
In Beirã you can find an old train station and railway and can experience the Rail Bike Marvão: info on https://www.railbikemarvao.com

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this gravel loop?
faqs.answer: The loop starts and ends in Castelo de Vide. You can find parking within the town, which serves as a convenient base for starting your ride. The Cycling Center of Castelo de Vide/Marvão also offers facilities that might include parking.

faqs.question: Is this gravel loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This gravel loop is graded as 'difficult' with a significant elevation gain of over 1800 meters and a distance of more than 72 km. While the sport type is 'mtb_easy', the overall difficulty suggests it's more suited for experienced gravel riders or those with good physical conditioning, rather than beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this gravel loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of unpaved rural roads and tracks, with some sections potentially including technical singletracks. You can expect rolling hills, offering a mix of climbs and descents. The route traverses through cork oak and holm oak forests, open fields, and areas with ancient megalithic sites.

faqs.question: What are the 'erratic boulders' mentioned in the trail name?
faqs.answer: The 'erratic boulders' likely refer to the numerous ancient megalithic sites, such as menhirs and dolmens, that are characteristic of the region around Castelo de Vide. These impressive stone formations are a unique historical and cultural feature you'll encounter along the route.

faqs.question: What points of interest will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: This loop offers a rich historical and natural experience. You'll pass through the charming town of Castelo de Vide, the historic Marvão Walled Town and Castle, and the settlement of Beirã. Additionally, you'll encounter ancient megalithic sites and enjoy views of cork oak forests and rolling hills within the Serra de São Mamede Natural Park.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this gravel loop?
faqs.answer: The Alto Alentejo region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions outside of the peak summer heat. Spring (March to May) and Autumn (September to November) are typically ideal, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable if visiting during that season.

faqs.question: Is this gravel loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through natural areas, it's important to check local regulations for cycling with dogs, especially within the Serra de São Mamede Natural Park. Generally, dogs are allowed on trails but should be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure safety for all users.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route?
faqs.answer: This gravel loop is located within the Parque Natural da Serra de São Mamede. Typically, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public trails within the park. However, always respect park regulations, especially regarding waste disposal and wildlife protection.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with several official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Percurso Pedestre dos Galegos, Percurso Pedestre das Carreiras, Percurso Pedestre de Marvão, and various sections of the Calçada Medieval and Calçada Romana, among others.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food and drink along the route?
faqs.answer: The loop passes through several settlements, including Castelo de Vide, Marvão, and Beirã. These towns offer opportunities to refuel at cafes, restaurants, or local shops. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially for longer stretches between villages.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this gravel loop?
faqs.answer: Given the loop's length and difficulty, bring plenty of water, energy snacks, and essential bike repair tools. Appropriate gravel biking attire, including a helmet, gloves, and padded shorts, is recommended. Sunscreen, sunglasses, and a charged phone with the route downloaded are also crucial for a safe and enjoyable ride.
